  ### World Labs
  
  2w
  
  [Fei-Fei Li reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/world-labs_with-chisel-you-can-keep-the-same-structure-activity-7442604952159592448-gaVn)
  
  With Chisel, you can keep the same structure and explore completely different worlds.
  Ancient tomb 🏺 → lava cavern 🌋 → overgrown ruins 🌿 Same layout, totally different feel.
  Chisel lets you block out a 3D layout and generate a detailed world on top, so you define the space first and then bring it to life.
  In this demo, we used that workflow to build and explore a range of environments with off-axis projection, all running in the browser.
  Pipeline:
  → Block out structure in Blender or directly in Chisel
  → Use Chisel to generate a detailed environment from that layout
  → Bring it into Three.js and use Spark for rendering
  → Use Mediapipe for face tracking to enable off-axis projection
  You can go from a rough spatial idea to something you can actually move around in very quickly.
  Learn more about Chisel: [https://lnkd.in/gS4sXtvM](https://lnkd.in/gS4sXtvM?trk=public_profile__posts-text)
  
  [public\_profile\_\_posts](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/world-labs_with-chisel-you-can-keep-the-same-structure-activity-7442604952159592448-gaVn)

* [Fei-Fei Li reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fei-fei-li-4541247?trk=public_profile__posts_face-pile-cta)
  
    + [Report this post](https://www.linkedin.com/uas/login?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fwenlongh_what-representation-enables-open-world-robot-activity-7441998081681911808-PWPa&trk=public_profile__posts_ellipsis-menu-semaphore-sign-in-redirect&guestReportContentType=POST&_f=guest-reporting)
  
  [Fei-Fei Li reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/wenlongh_what-representation-enables-open-world-robot-activity-7441998081681911808-PWPa)
  
  [Wenlong Huang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/wenlongh)
  
  ### Wenlong Huang
  
  2w
  
  [Fei-Fei Li reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/wenlongh_what-representation-enables-open-world-robot-activity-7441998081681911808-PWPa)
  
  What representation enables open-world robot manipulation from generated videos?
  Introducing Dream2Flow, our recent work that bridges video generation and robot control with 3D object flow.
  🌐 [dream2flow.github.io](http://dream2flow.github.io?trk=public_profile__posts-text) by [Stanford University](https://www.linkedin.com/school/stanford-university/?trk=public_profile__posts-text) 🔹Robot manipulation is about inducing changes in an environment through actions.
  We observe that video models (e.g., Veo) excel at producing plausible object motions from an in-the-wild image and language instructions.
  Intriguingly, these motions are more physically realistic when the actor is human rather than robot, likely because the internet contains far more human interaction data than robot data.
  🔹But how do we turn those generated videos into low-level robot actions?
  This is a nuanced question beyond simple retargeting, because strategies taken by a human may not work on a robot. 🔹We propose Dream2Flow, which uses 3D object flow to separate what should happen in the scene from how a robot should realize it.
  We extract this flow from generated videos using off-the-shelf vision models, then use it as a shared objective for both trajectory optimization and reinforcement learning.
  🔹Dream2Flow can perform a range of in-the-wild tasks zero-shot with trajectory optimization, including manipulation of rigid, articulated, and deformable objects.
  The robot plans by asking a counterfactual question using a dynamics model (either heuristics-based or learned): if I take this action, will the scene evolve toward the desired 3D flow?
  🔹Using as reward for RL, Dream2Flow enables different embodiments to discover emergent behaviors that achieve the same effect (e.g., base motion of the robot dog).
  Dream2Flow unifies these behaviors through a shared task interface and unifies model-free and model-based methods around a shared tracking goal.
  🔹By leveraging purely off-the-shelf video models, Dream2Flow also allows generalization to different object instances, backgrounds, and camera viewpoints.
  It is also surprisingly steerable: different language instructions in the same scene can induce different desired behaviors.
  🔹World modeling encodes rich priors about not only environment dynamics but also behaviors within it. It is immensely useful for robotics, yet we are only scratching the surface of understanding it.
  The project was led by [Karthik Dharmarajan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/karthik-dharmarajan?trk=public_profile__posts-text) and has been a year in the making, along with the rest of the team [Jiajun Wu](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jiajunwu?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Fei-Fei Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fei-fei-li-4541247?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , and [Ruohan Zhang](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruohan-zhang-2219b782?trk=public_profile__posts-text) . [Karthik Dharmarajan](https://www.linkedin.com/in/karthik-dharmarajan?trk=public_profile__posts-text) will also be joining UC Berkeley as a PhD student this fall!
  Website: [dream2flow.github.io](http://dream2flow.github.io?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Paper: [https://lnkd.in/gpwP2hkT](https://lnkd.in/gpwP2hkT?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Code: [https://lnkd.in/gvJZTxaP](https://lnkd.in/gvJZTxaP?trk=public_profile__posts-text)
  
  [public\_profile\_\_posts](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/wenlongh_what-representation-enables-open-world-robot-activity-7441998081681911808-PWPa)

  ### World Labs
  
  1mo
  
  [Fei-Fei Li reposted this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/world-labs_70-hackers-joined-us-in-sf-for-the-first-ever-activity-7435409354020909056-L6Fk)
  
  70 hackers joined us in SF for the first-ever World Labs Hackathon.
  In just 3.5 hours, 32 teams used Marble for projects ranging from robotics sims and agents to AR/VR interfaces, games, art experiences, and real estate tools. Check out what they built 👇
  🥇 Musée du Monde — an interactive museum where visitors step inside famous paintings. From Van Gogh’s bedroom to worlds inspired by Vermeer and Matisse, each artwork becomes a fully explorable 3D environment generated with Marble. [https://lnkd.in/gPbm3JrV](https://lnkd.in/gPbm3JrV?trk=public_profile__posts-text) 🥈 Jar of Marbles — a semantic interface for organizing Marble worlds into clusters like realistic vs geometric or vast vs intimate, explored using computer vision gesture controls. [https://lnkd.in/g3uMeGeK](https://lnkd.in/g3uMeGeK?trk=public_profile__posts-text) 🥉 Augmented Virtuality Room - uses Marble worlds as simulation environments for robotics. A robot explores with raycast LiDAR, builds a semantic map, and comments on the environment in real time. [https://lnkd.in/gt69PpXS](https://lnkd.in/gt69PpXS?trk=public_profile__posts-text) 👏 Honorable mentions: Marble Estates, Holocrime, Inkling, and Blocks.
  Huge thank you to all the hackers who joined us for WL-HACK 01.
  This event was about bringing together builders excited about the future of world models and spatial intelligence. The creativity and range of ideas we saw in just a few hours was incredible.
  
  [public\_profile\_\_posts](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/world-labs_70-hackers-joined-us-in-sf-for-the-first-ever-activity-7435409354020909056-L6Fk)

  Today we’re adding 7,000 free credits for World API users 🌎 New signups get them automatically, and existing API accounts will receive them as well.
  In just a few weeks since launch, people are already building:
  • Apps that convert real-world spaces into digital cousins in minutes
  • Full 3D worlds generated on demand and loaded directly into simulators like MuJoCo
  • Architectural workflows that transform sketches into explorable browser-based environments
  Learn more about the World API: [https://lnkd.in/ggmsu-x5](https://lnkd.in/ggmsu-x5?trk=public_profile__posts-text) Start building today: [https://lnkd.in/g5wVkXDE](https://lnkd.in/g5wVkXDE?trk=public_profile__posts-text)

  [Andrew Anagnost](https://www.linkedin.com/in/andrewanagnost)
  
  ### Andrew Anagnost
  
  1mo
  
  [Fei-Fei Li shared this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/fei-fei-li-4541247_lets-build-activity-7429937348685049857-FBcz)
  
  Today, we are announcing Autodesk’s investment in World Labs, co-founded by Dr. [Fei-Fei Li](https://www.linkedin.com/in/fei-fei-li-4541247?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Justin Johnson](https://www.linkedin.com/in/justin-johnson-41b43664?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , [Christoph Lassner](https://www.linkedin.com/in/christoph-lassner-475a669b?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , and  [Ben Mildenhall](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ben-mildenhall-86b4739b?trk=public_profile__posts-text) , and their exceptional team pushing AI toward its next frontier. This investment reflects a belief I hold deeply: the next chapter of AI will be defined by systems that can understand and reason about the physical world, not just language. 
   
  Designing and making the world requires AI that understands space, structure, physics, materials, and time. [World Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/world-labs?trk=public_profile__posts-text) is taking a distinct approach to AI that can create and reason about realistic 3D environments that persist and evolve. Combined with Autodesk’s domain expertise, we’re forging a different path for next generation design, simulation, and digital twin workflows. 
   
  Autodesk has spent decades building deep knowledge in geometry, simulation, and the workflows used by the people who design and build. We have already developed models that understand sketches, editable geometry, and the form, fit, and function required to make things real. Horizontal models cannot solve these problems alone. As world models mature, vertical expertise will remain essential, and Autodesk is uniquely positioned to bring these layers together. 
   
  The world is facing urgent global capacity challenges. We need more housing, more infrastructure, and more resilient systems, delivered faster and more sustainably. When AI can truly understand physical reality, we unlock new levels of efficiency and collaboration across the industries that build the world. 
   
  I am excited about what we will build together with Fei-Fei and the team at World Labs, and what this means for our customers shaping the future every day. 
   
  Learn more here: [https://lnkd.in/gt2vCRng](https://lnkd.in/gt2vCRng?trk=public_profile__posts-text)
  
  [public\_profile\_\_posts](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/fei-fei-li-4541247_lets-build-activity-7429937348685049857-FBcz)

  [Lukasz Mirocha, PhD](https://hk.linkedin.com/in/lukaszmirocha)
  
  ### Lukasz Mirocha, PhD
  
  3d
  
  [Fei-Fei Li liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/lukaszmirocha_lichtfeld-activity-7447901154505814016-QIQ3)
  
  "Professor, what if I want a Gaussian Splat of something that doesn't exist in the real world?" Me: just type [marble.worldlabs.ai](http://marble.worldlabs.ai?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) \- and we'll take it from there.
  We've come a long way this semester. My students started by capturing real-world objects using phones, mirrorless cameras, and SLAM scanners, building spatial reconstructions with tools like [Scaniverse](https://www.linkedin.com/company/scaniverse?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [KIRI Engine - 3D Scanner](https://ca.linkedin.com/company/kiri-innov?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , [\](https://www.linkedin.com/signup/cold-join?session_redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Ffeed%2Fhashtag%2Flichtfeld&trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , and [XGRIDS](https://www.linkedin.com/company/xgrids?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) SLAM scanners. 📱🔬
  Now, we are focusing on harnessing the power (and limitations) of generative AI in the context of 3D Gaussian Splatting. ✨
  🌍 Students will be working with Marble by [World Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/world-labs?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) , learning to use text and image prompts to generate splat-based 3D worlds from scratch, and diving into Chisel, Marble's experimental tool for blocking out the structure of enclosed spaces before letting AI fill in the detail.
  🎓 All of this is part of my Arts Tech Practices I (Making Senses) course this semester at [Hong Kong Baptist University](https://hk.linkedin.com/school/hong-kong-baptist-university/?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) \- among the first university courses worldwide teaching hand-on 3D Gaussian Splatting in creative media and industry contexts. The training and education activities of [Opus Lab 3D](https://hk.linkedin.com/company/opus-lab-3d?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) are just beginning.
  Special mention goes to [Jonathan Stephens](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jonathanstephens?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) for his 30-minute video in which he tries to recreate his kitchen in Marble. My students found it equally amusing and informative.
  Huge thank you to [World Labs](https://www.linkedin.com/company/world-labs?trk=public_profile__reactions-text) for partnering with us this term.
  
  [public\_profile\_\_reactions](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/lukaszmirocha_lichtfeld-activity-7447901154505814016-QIQ3)

  ### Michael J. Black
  
  1w
  
  [Fei-Fei Li liked this](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/michael-j-black-41751114_tomorrow-i-start-a-new-full-time-position-activity-7444748981143912449-sK3L)
  
  Tomorrow, I start a new full-time position as VP Digital Human Research at Epic Games. (And no, this isn’t an April Fools’ joke.) Today, I’m taking early retirement from Max Planck and will become an Emeritus Director.
  As an Emeritus Director, I will continue to supervise my remaining students, oversee ongoing projects, and wind down my department over the next couple of years, which is a normal process when a director retires.
  Being a Max Planck director is the best academic job in the world and it has been my great honor to co-found and help build the MPI for Intelligent Systems. I love the institute deeply.
  But my statutory retirement age was looming and I’m not done yet. I’ve been working on capturing and modeling human movement for 30 years and it has gone from a fringe topic to something that works robustly and has immense potential.
  With AI today, scale matters, and achieving that scale increasingly requires industry. In particular, digital humans are moving from research prototypes to foundational technology across industries. At this point in my career, I want to get this technology into the hands of millions of users, while pushing the frontier of digital humans.
  I know that this is a challenging time for the games industry. Such times are precisely when people get creative, the industry is open to change, and real innovation can take root.
  Epic Games is the ideal place for this. The talent is deep, there is a compelling vision for how games will evolve, and the commitment is clear. I’m excited to join them and the rest of the Meshcapade team.
  There is no good way to express on social media the depth of my gratitude to the Max Planck Society, my co-directors, students, post docs, and staff. It has been an amazing 15-year journey because of you. Thank you.

  ### World Labs

  This entire cyberpunk world was built by a single creator. 🤯 100 million Gaussian splats. Nearly every surface and structure generated in Marble.
  We’re entering an era where individuals can build entire worlds. 🌎
  AI tools shouldn’t be linear. They should empower small teams, and even solo creators, to dream bigger through iterative creation, editing, and exploration.
  That’s the kind of workflow that unlocks entirely new creative possibilities. Shoutout to the world builder José Tijerin for his amazing work 🤩 more worlds loading.
